title: Contract and docs still describe legacy or missing execution surfaces that do not match the real planner
root_cause: Static contracts/docs drifted away from actual planner output and wrapper paths.
failure_mode: Plans and docs look valid while the real executable path is different or missing.
why_it_matters: Operator and governance trust fail before runtime, and stage execution can fail late on missing wrappers.
suggested_remediation: Regenerate or rewrite the registry/docs from actual planner output, and make the registry reject missing scripts.

title: Artifact status can go green without proving output truth
root_cause: Manifests and audits trust terminal status paperwork more than produced artifacts.
failure_mode: Stages, runs, and generated audits can report success while outputs are empty, malformed, or missing.
why_it_matters: False-green artifacts undermine reproducibility, promotion trust, and runtime safety.
suggested_remediation: Require success manifests and audits to prove outputs exist and validate against the real contract source before promoting status.

title: Same-bar and horizon-boundary leakage remains possible through config and evaluator paths
root_cause: Entry-lag invariants and split-window semantics are not enforced uniformly across proposal, experiment, and evaluator entrypoints.
failure_mode: Entry-lag 0 and boundary-crossing horizons can survive into scored hypotheses labeled as validation-safe.
why_it_matters: Statistical integrity is compromised at the core discovery surface.
suggested_remediation: Enforce lag >= 1 everywhere and classify/drop events based on realized event windows, not entry bars alone.

title: Placebo and confirmatory falsification paths are not evaluation-slice faithful
root_cause: Placebo timestamps and confirmatory placebo filtering use convenience logic rather than a true evaluation-slice null model.
failure_mode: Train rows and prior-event timestamps influence confirmatory placebo outcomes.
why_it_matters: Negative controls can look stronger or weaker for the wrong reason.
suggested_remediation: Generate true bar-shift placebos and filter placebo frames to evaluation rows before gating.

title: Economic identity and cost semantics are inconsistent across search, bridge, eval, and confirmatory paths
root_cause: Cost units, carry handling, and confirmatory structural keys are inconsistent or optional across code paths.
failure_mode: The same strategy can have different after-cost economics or still compare as compatible despite changed cost contracts.
why_it_matters: Promotion and confirmatory decisions can rest on inconsistent economics rather than genuine edge stability.
suggested_remediation: Standardize cost units, separate transaction cost from funding carry, and require cost-identity fields in confirmatory comparison.

title: Runtime monitor-only and provenance gates are not hard safety barriers
root_cause: Runtime gating checks are split, metadata-only, or based on weak booleans.
failure_mode: Monitor-only can mutate the venue, incubating strategies can still trade, and forged StrategyResult payloads can reach live submission.
why_it_matters: The live runtime has direct operator and capital risk.
suggested_remediation: Enforce mode/provenance/incubation at the OMS boundary and remove venue mutation from monitor_only unless explicitly renamed and documented.

title: Live runtime state and postflight audits can act on stale or malformed data
root_cause: Local fill state is not merged into LiveStateStore promptly, and postflight checks skip real normalization.
failure_mode: Safety decisions use stale positions while postflight artifacts can pass on malformed event rows.
why_it_matters: Replay/live parity and kill-switch behavior are less trustworthy than the runtime artifacts imply.
suggested_remediation: Update local state on fills or merge with snapshots, and normalize events before postflight auditing.

title: Ontology and detector governance use multiple conflicting event authorities
root_cause: Runtime, detectors, and generated audits do not all use the same canonical registry source.
failure_mode: Aliases split canonical episodes, detector ids exist outside the active ontology, and coverage artifacts disagree on event inventory.
why_it_matters: Research and runtime can talk about different event universes while audits still pass.
suggested_remediation: Make unified registry + compiled registry the sole ontology source for runtime and audit tooling, and normalize aliases before deconfliction.

title: Blueprint/spec/runtime boundaries lose provenance and determinism
root_cause: Runtime rebuild logic drops lineage fields, clustering uses non-stable hashing, and runtime contracts require undeclared data fields.
failure_mode: Executable strategies are not stable or provenance-preserving across serialization and runtime.
why_it_matters: Blueprints cannot be audited or reproduced reliably into engine/runtime behavior.
suggested_remediation: Round-trip the full lineage, use stable clustering keys, and narrow runtime data requirements to strategy-declared dependencies.

title: Spec and config defaults are internally contradictory or disconnected from runtime consumers
root_cause: Multiple loaders and legacy surfaces encode different schema expectations for the same knobs and labels.
failure_mode: Defaults, allowed labels, and runtime consistency checks disagree or no-op.
why_it_matters: Operator-facing YAML is not a reproducible source of truth for runtime/search behavior.
suggested_remediation: Collapse onto one authoritative loader stack and reject unsupported or self-contradictory spec fields in CI and runtime.

title: Reliability and CI protect artifact shape more than inference integrity
root_cause: PR CI omits key deterministic smoke/PIT/synthetic-truth canaries, and some validators check artifacts rather than real inference chains.
failure_mode: CLI contract regressions, synthetic inference regressions, and PIT guard drift can merge while CI stays green.
why_it_matters: The repo can look healthy in CI while the highest-risk research and runtime invariants are unprotected.
suggested_remediation: Move a minimal deterministic smoke + PIT + synthetic-truth canary set into PR gating and test the public CLI entrypoints directly.

title: Operator surface contains undocumented bypass and mutation paths
root_cause: Maintained docs describe a narrow surface, but project/scripts contains additional mutating or bypass tools with weaker contracts.
failure_mode: Operators can follow a documented command and hit hidden prerequisites or use undocumented scripts that bypass lineage, compatibility, or governance.
why_it_matters: Operator mistakes become more likely exactly where the repo claims disciplined workflow is important.
suggested_remediation: Prune or govern undocumented scripts, unify artifact path helpers, and document environment contracts for maintained entrypoints.
